'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
- '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
- '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
- '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
- '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 burial mounds''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

| burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
- '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
- '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
- '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
- '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 burial mounds''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
- '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
- '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
- '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
- '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 burial mounds''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

- '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
- '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
- '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
- '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 burial mounds''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

{{Infobox kofun
| name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun
| image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]]
| caption = Full view (March 2021)
| location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Cultural Properties of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>
| coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}}
| type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| size = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}
| burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| artifacts =
| companion =
| construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/>
| occupant = Unknown
| mausoleum =
| designation = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}
| map = Japan Tochigi
| icon = Enpun
| label =
| label_position= bottom
}}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient burial mound (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
- '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
- '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
- '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
- '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 burial mounds''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.

No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
- '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
- '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
- '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
- '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
- '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
- '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
- '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
- '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|円墳" to "|円墳|1=Round Kofun"
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun
| name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun
| image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]]
| caption = Full view (March 2021)
| location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Cultural Properties of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>
| coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}}
| type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| size = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}
| burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| artifacts =
| companion =
| construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/>
| occupant = Unknown
| mausoleum =
| designation = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}
| map = Japan Tochigi
| icon = Enpun
| label =
| label_position= bottom
}}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun
| name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun
| image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]]
| caption = Full view (March 2021)
| location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Cultural Properties of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>
| coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}}
| type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| size = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}
| burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| artifacts =
| companion =
| construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/>
| occupant = Unknown
| mausoleum =
| designation = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}
| map = Japan Tochigi
| icon = Enpun
| label =
| label_position= bottom
}}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming Map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=16593 Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun - Tochigi Navi]

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). == Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox kofun
| name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun
| image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]]
| caption = Full view (March 2021)
| location = [[Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and other_designation of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>
| coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}}
| type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| size = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}
| burial = [[Yokoana-style stone chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>
| artifacts =
| companion =

| occupant = Unknown

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was designated a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.

A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
