Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)
* '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}

Nearby kofun sites include:
* '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east)
* '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun)
* '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east)

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:下栗大塚古墳 up to rev 90219729
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 下栗大塚古墳 |画像 =[[ファイル:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] |画像キャプション =全景（2021年3月） |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]下栗町1382{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=下栗大塚古墳|work=宇都宮の歴史と文化財|publisher=宇都宮市歴史文化資源活用 推進協議会|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> |位置 = |緯度度 =36 |緯度分 =31 |緯度秒 =39.9 |経度度 =139 |経度分 =53 |経度秒 =52.9 |ISO = JP-09 |形状 = [[円墳]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> |規模 = 直径43.5 [[メートル|m]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}} |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（推定）{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[古墳時代]]終末期（[[7世紀]]）<ref name="u8s"/> |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 宇都宮市指定史跡{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}} |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 =墳頂に丸山稲荷大明神あり |地図 =Japan Tochigi |地図2 =Japan Tochigi |アイコン =円墳 |ラベル = |ラベル位置 =bottom }} '''下栗大塚古墳'''（しもぐりおおつかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]下栗町にある[[古墳]]{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}。
== 概説 == [[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]低地にある2段築成の[[円墳]]で、[[直径]]は約43.5 [[メートル|m]]、高さは約6.5 mである{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}。中段に幅4 - 5 mほどの平坦面が周囲をめぐっている<ref name="u8s"/>。墳丘は全面に樹木が茂っており{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=下栗大塚古墳|publisher=宇都宮市教育センター|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>、水田に囲まれているため、周囲から目立つ<ref name="ueis"/>。頂上に丸山稲荷大明神という[[神社]]がある{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>。
埋葬施設は不明であるが、南側に若干の崩れがあることから、南に開口した[[横穴式石室]]と推定される{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}。築造時期も不明であるが、[[埴輪]]や[[葺石]]が見つかっていないことから{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>、[[7世紀]]の[[古墳時代]]終末期（[[飛鳥時代]]）に造られたものと考えられる<ref name="u8s"/>。
[[1995年]]（平成7年）[[3月22日]]に<ref name="u8s"/>、宇都宮市指定[[史跡]]となった{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}。下栗大塚古墳愛護会が保護活動を行っている<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=宇都宮の環境 （平成25年度 環境状況報告書）|publisher=宇都宮市環境部環境政策課|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>。
[[栃木県庁]]が登録した「とちぎ健康づくりロード」の「横川フラワーロードコース」では、下栗大塚古墳が最後のチェックポイントに設定されている<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=横川フラワーロードコース|work=とちぎ健康づくりロード|publisher=栃木県保健福祉部健康増進課健康長寿推進班|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>。
== 民話 == 下栗大塚古墳の頂上にある丸山稲荷大明神{{sfn|塙|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>には、次のような[[民話]]が残されている{{sfn|伊藤|2011|p=86-88}}。 [[ファイル:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|頂上に鎮座する丸山稲荷大明神]] {{Cquote|昔々、大塚の周辺で[[凶作]]が続き、村人らは五穀豊穣を願って大塚の上に[[稲荷神社|お稲荷様]]を建てることにした。建立の許可を得るため、村人の代表3人が[[京都|京の都]]に赴くが、「古墳は[[お上]]のものであり、何も建ててはならぬ」と拒否されてしまった。そこで3人のうちの1人が「古墳と言うからダメなんだ」と知恵を働かせ、地元の呼び名である「大塚」にお稲荷様を祀りたいと願い出ると許可を得ることができた。しかしお上は10万両を出せと要求し、3人は諦めかけるが、「これ以上は出せない」と有り金の10両を差し出して懇願した。当時、お上もお金に困っていたと見え、10両で承諾したばかりか、「[[正一位]]稲荷大明神」という立派な名前も授けた。これには村人代表たちも大喜びで、[[伏見稲荷大社|伏見稲荷]]にお参りしてから帰路に就いた。村に戻ると大塚のてっぺんに「正一位稲荷大明神」を祀り、以後[[豊作]]が続いた。}}
== 周辺環境 == 下栗町を含む宇都宮市横川地区（旧[[横川村]]）は歴史文化資源が豊富な地域であり、市の指定文化財である下栗大塚古墳は横川地区を代表する史跡である<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=令和元年度 まちづくり懇談会実施結果報告書 （横川地区）|publisher=宇都宮市総合政策部広報広聴課|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>。特に下栗町は古墳の多い町で、下栗大塚古墳の東へ約700 mのところに大塚神社古墳、大塚神社古墳から南南東へ約1.2 [[キロメートル|km]]のところに猿山城跡内古墳群、さらに東へ進むと東原古墳、その北方に天王山古墳群がある{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=154-155}}。猿山城跡内古墳群は、その名の通り猿山城跡にある11基の古墳群であり、猿山城は古墳をほとんど破壊せずに防御に活用していた{{sfn|塙|2008|pp=154-155}}。
下栗大塚古墳の近くを'''下栗大塚通り'''（しもぐりおおつかどおり）が通っている。[[宇都宮市の道路愛称事業|宇都宮市の制定した道路愛称]]による下栗大塚通りは、川田町の[[栃木県道・茨城県道35号宇都宮結城線|上三川街道]]を起点とし、下栗町の[[栃木県道46号宇都宮真岡線]]を終点とする通りである<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=うつのみや道路愛称マップ|publisher=宇都宮市建設部道路管理課管理グループ|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref><ref name="ua2">{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapura.pdf|title=うつのみや愛称道路・坂一覧|publisher=宇都宮市建設部道路管理課管理グループ|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>。この区間は宇都宮市道1393号と一致する<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/010/039/30zumen24.pdf|title=宇都宮市認定市道/図面24|publisher=宇都宮市総合政策部情報政策課|date=2019-01-11|quote=この作品は[[クリエイティブ・コモンズ]] 表示 2.1 国際 ライセンスの下に提供されています。|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/010/039/30zumen25.pdf|title=宇都宮市認定市道/図面25|publisher=宇都宮市総合政策部情報政策課|date=2019-01-11|quote=この作品はクリエイティブ・コモンズ 表示 2.1 国際 ライセンスの下に提供されています。|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=伊藤芳昌|chapter=大塚のお稲荷様|editor=栃木の民話語り かまどの会 編著|title=親と子で語る うつのみやの民話|date=2011-02-25|publisher=[[随想舎]]|pages=86-88|isbn=978-4-88748-234-0|ref={{sfnref|伊藤|2011}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=塙静夫|authorlink=塙静夫|title=うつのみや歴史探訪 史跡案内九十九景|date=2008-09-27|publisher=随想舎|page=287|isbn=978-4-88748-179-4|ref={{sfnref|塙|2008}}}}

== 外部リンク == * [http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/sizen16/yokotou/html/ootuka.htm 下栗大塚古墳] - 宇都宮市教育センター * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=16593 下栗大塚古墳 - 宇都宮市の古墳・遺跡] - 栃ナビ!（ヤマゼンコミュニケイションズ）

下栗大塚古墳の近くを'''下栗大塚通り'''（しもぐりおおつかどおり）が通っている。[[宇都宮市の道路愛称事業|宇都宮市の制定した道路愛称]]による下栗大塚通りは、川田町の[[栃木県道・茨城県道35号宇都宮結城線|上三川街道]]を起点とし、下栗町の[[栃木県道46号宇都宮真岡線]]を終点とする通りである<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=うつのみや道路愛称マップ|publisher=宇都宮市建設部道路管理課管理グループ|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref><ref name="ua2">{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapura.pdf|title=うつのみや愛称道路・坂一覧|publisher=宇都宮市建設部道路管理課管理グループ|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>。この区間は宇都宮市道1393号と一致する<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/010/039/30zumen24.pdf|title=宇都宮市認定市道/図面24|publisher=宇都宮市総合政策部情報政策課|date=2019-01-11|quote=この作品は[[クリエイティブ・コモンズ]] 表示 2.1 国際 ライセンスの下に提供されています。|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/010/039/30zumen25.pdf|title=宇都宮市認定市道/図面25|publisher=宇都宮市総合政策部情報政策課|date=2019-01-11|quote=この作品はクリエイティブ・コモンズ 表示 2.1 国際 ライセンスの下に提供されています。|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | caption = Full view (March 2021) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Historic Site of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | area = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | burial goods = | companion = | construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/> | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label= bottom }}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | caption = Full view (March 2021) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Historic Site of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | area = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | burial goods = | companion = | construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/> | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label= bottom }}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | caption = Full view (March 2021) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Historic Site of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | area = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | burial goods = | companion = | construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/> | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label= bottom }}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | caption = Full view (March 2021) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Historic Site of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | area = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | burial goods = | companion = | construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/> | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label= bottom }}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

{{Infobox kofun | name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | caption = Full view (March 2021) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Historic Site of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | area = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | burial goods = | companion = | construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/> | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label= bottom }}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."

The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox kofun | name = Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun | image = [[File:Shimoguri-Otsuka Kofun.jpg|250px]] | caption = Full view (March 2021) | location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi]], [[:en:Utsunomiya, Tochigi|Utsunomiya City]], Shimoguri-chō 1382{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s">{{cite web|url=https://utsunomiya-8story.jp/search_post/%e4%b8%8b%e6%a0%97%e5%a4%a7%e5%a1%9a%e5%8f%a4%e5%a2%b3%e3%80%80/|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|work=History and Historic Site of Utsunomiya|publisher=Utsunomiya City Historical and Cultural Resources Promotion Council|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> | coordinates = {{Coord|36|31|39.9|N|139|53|52.9|E|region:JP-09}} | type = [[Round kofun|Enpun]] (circular mound){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | area = Diameter 43.5 m{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | burial = [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu]] (presumed){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/> | burial goods = | companion = | construction = [[7th century]] (Final Kofun period)<ref name="u8s"/> | occupant = Unknown | mausoleum = | Historic Site = Utsunomiya City Designated Historic Site{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}} | map = Japan Tochigi | type = Enpun | map_dot_label = | map_dot_label= bottom }}
'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.
== Overview == Located in the '''Tagawa lowlands''', Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is a '''two-tiered {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|lt=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''' (*enpun*) with a '''diameter of approximately 43.5 meters''' and a '''height of about 6.5 meters'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine''' is located at the summit of the mound{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>.
The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.
The tumulus was Historic Site1 a '''historic site by Utsunomiya City''' on '''March 22, 1995''' (Heisei 7)<ref name="u8s"/>. The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
The kofun is also a '''checkpoint''' on '''Tochigi Prefecture’s Health Road Walking Route ("Yokokawa Flower Road Course")'''<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.kenko-choju.tochigi.jp/road/course/00136/|title=Yokokawa Flower Road Course|work=Tochigi Health Road|publisher=Tochigi Prefecture Health Promotion Division|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
== Folklore == A local '''folktale''' is associated with '''Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin''', the shrine atop Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis"/>:
[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

'''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun''' (下栗大塚古墳) is an ancient Kofun (*kofun*) located in '''Shimoguri, Utsunomiya City, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}.

A '''flat terrace''' measuring '''4–5 meters wide''' encircles the mound at mid-height<ref name="u8s"/>. The mound is '''covered with dense trees'''{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="ueis">{{cite web|url=http://www.ueis.ed.jp/kyouzai/h25_syakai/yokokawa/ootuka.html|title=Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun|publisher=Utsunomiya City Educational Center|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref> and stands '''prominently''' in the surrounding rice fields<ref name="ueis"/>. The burial facility remains unknown, but '''a slight collapse on the southern side''' suggests a '''yokoana-style stone chamber''' (*yokoana-shiki sekishitsu*) opening towards the south{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}. No '''haniwa''' or '''fukiishi''' (cobblestone coverings) have been discovered{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|p=155}}<ref name="u8s"/>, leading archaeologists to estimate its construction in the '''7th century''', during the '''final Kofun period''' (*Asuka period*)<ref name="u8s"/>.

The '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun Conservation Association''' actively preserves the site<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/005/319/kannkyou25.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Environmental Report (Heisei 25)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Environmental Policy Division|date=2014-11|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.

[[File:Maruyama Inari Daimyojin in Shimoguri, Utsunomiya.jpg|thumb|Maruyama Inari Daimyōjin shrine at the summit]]
{{Cquote|Long ago, a great famine plagued the lands surrounding Ōtsuka. Desperate for good harvests, the villagers decided to enshrine an '''Inari deity''' atop the mound. However, upon seeking permission from the imperial court in Kyoto, they were told: "Kofun belong to the government. Nothing may be built upon them." One of the villagers then had an idea: "Instead of calling it a kofun, let's call it by its local name—Ōtsuka!"
The court agreed but demanded a '''payment of 100,000 ryō''' in silver. The villagers were crestfallen, knowing they could never afford such a sum. However, one of them pleaded, "We can only offer 10 ryō—please accept it."
The court, also struggling financially at the time, surprisingly '''agreed''' and '''granted the villagers the right to enshrine their deity'''. Not only that, they bestowed the shrine with the prestigious title '''"Shōichii Inari Daimyōjin"''' (正一位稲荷大明神). Overjoyed, the villagers returned home, enshrined the deity atop Ōtsuka, and from that day forth, '''their fields flourished abundantly'''.}}
== Surrounding Area == The '''Yokokawa region of Utsunomiya City''', including Shimoguri, is rich in '''historical and cultural heritage'''. Shimoguri Ōtsuka Kofun is one of the '''most prominent historic sites''' in the area<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/022/949/yokokawamati.pdf|title=2019 Community Development Report (Yokokawa District)|publisher=Utsunomiya City Planning Department|date=2019-11-21|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
Nearby kofun sites include: * '''Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun''' (700m east) * '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' (1.2km south-southeast of Ōtsuka Shrine Kofun) * '''Higashihara Kofun''' (further east) * '''Tennozan Kofun Cluster''' (north of Higashihara Kofun){{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}
The '''Saruyama Castle Site Kofun Cluster''' consists of '''11 Kofuns''' integrated into the castle’s '''defensive structures''', rather than being destroyed{{sfn|Hanawa|2008|pp=154-155}}.
A local road, '''Shimoguri Ōtsuka Street''' (*Shimoguri Ōtsuka-dōri*), passes near the kofun. This street, named by the '''Utsunomiya City Road Naming Initiative''', connects '''Kawada Town’s Kamikawa Highway''' (Route 35) to '''Shimoguri Town’s Utsunomiya-Moka Highway''' (Route 46)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.city.utsunomiya.tochigi.jp/_res/projects/default_project/_page_/001/006/146/aisyomapomote.pdf|title=Utsunomiya Road Naming map|publisher=Utsunomiya City Roads Department|date=2016-04|accessdate=2021-03-12}}</ref>.
